description_long: This is a long, slender woodwind instrument with a dark reddish-brown body and a contrasting silver-gray bell at the bottom. The body features a series of metallic keys, rods, and levers, which are distributed along its length and appear to be made of brass or nickel. The instrument's upper section is adorned with additional keywork and a mouthpiece area, which is slightly more intricate in its mechanical design. The bell flares gently outward and is smooth and metallic, providing a visual and material contrast to the wooden body. The overall craftsmanship suggests a high-quality orchestral instrument, likely a bassoon or contrabassoon, with a complex key mechanism for advanced playability. The finish is glossy, highlighting the polished wood and metal components.
description: A tall, reddish-brown woodwind instrument with a silver-gray flared bell and intricate metallic keywork along its body. The polished finish and detailed keys suggest a high-quality orchestral flute.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the instrument, with the bell and most keywork visible along the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the bell and the arrangement of keys and rods on one side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back keywork and the straight alignment of the instrument's body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the metallic bell and the less prominent keywork on this side.
